jsonLdWeb.name: Candle Wax Calculator
jsonLdWeb.description: Calculate wax weight, fragrance oil, and dye amounts for candle making with support for multiple wax types and container sizes.

mainEntity.name: How much wax do I need for a candle?

acceptedAnswer.text: Multiply your container volume by the wax density for your type. For soy wax, one pound fills about 20 oz of container volume. A typical 8 oz candle needs roughly 6.4 oz (0.4 lb) of soy wax.
Fragrance load is the percentage of fragrance oil relative to the total wax weight. Most waxes support 6-10% fragrance load. Soy wax typically maxes at 10-12%, while paraffin can hold 6-10%.

mainEntity.name: What is the density of soy wax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Soy wax has a density of approximately 0.9 g/ml (or about 0.86 oz per fluid oz of volume). This means soy wax shrinks slightly when cooling, so containers may need a second pour.

mainEntity.name: How much dye do I use per pound of wax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Liquid dye typically uses 3-10 drops per pound of wax, while dye blocks use about 0.1-0.2 oz per pound. Start small and increase until you reach your desired color intensity.

mainEntity.name: Should I fill my candle container all the way?

acceptedAnswer.text: No. Fill containers to about 85-90% capacity to leave room for the wick and prevent overflow. This calculator accounts for this fill percentage automatically.

mainEntity.name: What's the difference between soy and paraffin wax?

acceptedAnswer.text: Soy wax is natural, burns cleaner, and has a lower melting point. Paraffin is petroleum-based, holds more fragrance, and produces brighter colors. Soy is denser so you need less by volume but more by weight.

howItWorks:
How the Candle Wax Calculator Works

This calculator determines how much wax, fragrance oil, and dye you need based on your container size and wax type.

Wax Weight = Container Volume × Fill % × Wax Density
  • Fill percentage (typically 85%) leaves room for the wick and prevents overflow
  • Wax density varies by type — soy is lighter than paraffin or beeswax
  • Fragrance oil is calculated as a percentage of the wax weight (6-10% typical)
  • Dye amount is estimated at ~0.1 oz per pound of wax for medium color

Example: An 8 oz container with soy wax at 85% fill = 8 × 0.85 × 0.86 = 5.85 oz of wax per candle.
faqHTML:
Frequently Asked Questions

How much wax do I need for a candle?

The amount depends on your container volume and wax type. Multiply the container volume by the fill percentage (85%) and the wax density. For soy wax, an 8 oz container needs about 5.8 oz of wax.

What is fragrance load percentage?

Fragrance load is the percentage of fragrance oil relative to wax weight. Most waxes support 6-10%. Going above the recommended maximum can cause sweating, poor burn, or safety issues.

What is the density of soy wax?

Soy wax has a density of approximately 0.86 oz per fluid ounce (0.90 g/ml). It is lighter than paraffin (0.90 oz/fl oz) and beeswax (0.96 oz/fl oz).

How much dye do I use per pound of wax?

For liquid dye, use 3-10 drops per pound. For dye blocks, approximately 0.1-0.2 oz per pound. Start light and increase for deeper colors.

Should I fill my candle container all the way?

No. Fill to 85-90% capacity to leave room for the wick, prevent overflow when the wax melts, and allow for a clean finish. This calculator accounts for fill percentage.

What's the difference between soy and paraffin wax?

Soy wax is plant-based, burns cleaner with less soot, and has a lower melting point. Paraffin is petroleum-based, provides better scent throw, and produces more vibrant colors. Coconut blends offer a middle ground.

sections.title: What Is the Candle Wax Calculator?
sections.body: The Candle Wax Calculator is a free online tool designed for crafters and makers who need quick, accurate calculations in the crafting and DIY space. By entering your container volume, number of candles, wax type, you get instant results including wax per candle, total wax needed, fragrance oil. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting wax per candle right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In crafting and DIY, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by crafters and makers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to plan your projects with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your container volume and need to find the right wax per candle. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.

howToSteps.title: Enter Your Container Volume
howToSteps.description: Start by entering your container volume — this is the primary input for the calculation.

howToSteps.title: Fill In Additional Details
howToSteps.description: Complete the remaining fields: number of candles, wax type, fragrance load, fill percentage. Each value refines the calculation for greater accuracy.

howToSteps.title: Click Calculate
howToSteps.description: Hit the Calculate button to run the numbers. Results appear instantly below.

howToSteps.title: Review Your Results
howToSteps.description: Check your wax per candle, total wax needed, fragrance oil. Use these figures to inform your next decision or compare against alternative scenarios.

tips: Double-check your container volume before calculating — even small input errors can significantly change your results.
Run the calculator with different values to compare scenarios and find the optimal approach for your situation.
Pay attention to both wax per candle and total wax needed — they work together to give you the full picture.
Bookmark this page for quick access next time you need to plan your projects.
If you're unsure about your fill percentage, start with a conservative estimate and adjust from there.

faq.question: Is the Candle Wax Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard crafting and DIY formulas trusted by crafters and makers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

faq.question: What container volume should I enter?
faq.answer: Enter the most accurate container volume value you have available. If you're estimating, use a conservative figure. You can always run the calculator again with different values to see how changes affect the results.

faq.question: Can I use this calculator on my phone?
faq.answer: Yes, the Candle Wax Calculator is fully responsive and works on any device — phone, tablet, or desktop. No app download needed.

faq.question: What does wax per candle mean in my results?
faq.answer: The wax per candle value represents the primary output of the calculation based on your inputs. Use it as a baseline figure for your crafting and DIY planning and decision-making.
